title: Board and Batten Media Console
summary: A media console with two drawers and two board-and-batten doors on 2x2 legs. Cottage styling over honest 1x12 casework.
description:
This console dresses up simple 1x12 casework with a board-and-batten front — the vertical batten strips on the doors and the chunky 2x2 legs and trim give it a cottage weight that plain plywood wouldn't. Underneath it's approachable: a 1x12 box, two drawers, and two doors, joined with pocket holes or countersunk screws.

The framework matters more than any single joint. The side trim, the legs, and the front/back 2x2 trim each add a layer of rigidity, so even though the box is nailable, the finished piece ends up genuinely stout. Attach the legs flush to the inside of the box and the outside of the side trim; fasten the front/back trim the same way and tie the legs to the trim — that's what stiffens it.

The drawers are lifted straight from the site's nightstand plans (intentionally identical), and the same rule applies: measure the exact finished opening and build the drawers and guides to it, shimming the bottom guides ~1/8" for a perfect-fitting drawer. Cut the door and drawer face parts to your real openings, not the drawing.

tools.note: Or countersunk screws / nails with lots of glue.

materials.name: 2x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs, front/back trim, drawer/door trim, drawer guides.

materials.name: 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Side trim, door battens, drawer guides.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood
materials.note: Door backs and optional console back.

cutList.note: Cut to the width of your 1x12.
Cut to the width of your 1x12.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 19-1/2", one top at 57-1/2", two center partitions at 18-3/4", one box bottom at 54-1/2" and two drawer bottoms at 18-3/4" from the 1x12. Cut four side trim at 11-1/2", one top trim back at 57-1/2" and six door battens / stiles at 11-3/8" from the 1x2. Cut four legs at 23-1/4", four front/back trim at 54-1/2" and four drawer/door trim at 16-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut one top trim front at 57-1/2" and four door rails at 15-3/8" from the 1x3. Cut four drawer front/back at 20-1/4" and four drawer sides at 11-1/2" from the 1x8. Cut two door backs at 16-1/4" from the 1/4" plywood. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to three 8 ft 1x12, two 8 ft 1x2, four 8 ft 2x2, two 8 ft 1x3 and one 12 ft 1x8. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Attach the sides to the side trim
steps.body: Attach each side to its side trim as shown — do two. Fastening from the inside hides the screw holes. This is the first of several layers that stiffen the finished piece.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build the box with the sides, top, bottom, and center partitions. Pocket screws (1-1/4") are cleanest, but countersunk screws and glue or even nails with lots of glue work — just make sure the fasteners are appropriate. Check for square.

steps.title: Add the legs and front/back trim
steps.body: Attach the legs flush to the inside of the box and the outside of the side trim, with finish nails or screws and glue or pocket holes from inside. Then add the front/back 2x2 trim the same way, and fasten the legs to the trim. Each layer adds real rigidity.

steps.title: Finish the top trim
steps.body: Add the top front and back trim. If using pocket holes, predrill holes in the top 1x12 to attach the trim from underneath so nothing shows on top.

steps.title: Build the drawers and guides
steps.body: Build the two drawers with pocket screws or 2" screws and glue. Place the bottom drawer guides first, shimming up ~1/8" as needed for a perfect-fitting drawer, then set the side guides. Measure the exact opening of your cabinet and build the drawers to it — don't trust the drawing.

steps.title: Build the doors, then finish
steps.body: Build the two board-and-batten doors from the 1x3 rails, 1x2 battens, and 1/4" plywood backs, sized to your real openings. Hang them, add knobs or handles to doors and drawers, and finish — this style takes paint and primer beautifully, but stain works too.

images.alt: A media console with two drawers and two board-and-batten doors on chunky legs
